statement.markdown: Given a non-negative integer `n`, generate the Fibonacci sequence up to the `n`-th number. The sequence is defined as: `F(0) = 0`, `F(1) = 1`, and `F(i) = F(i-1) + F(i-2)` for `i > 1`. Implement a function that returns the Fibonacci sequence as a list of integers.
statement.inputFormat: An integer `n` representing the length of the Fibonacci sequence to generate.
statement.outputFormat: A list of integers representing the Fibonacci sequence up to the `n`-th number.
statement.notes: The sequence should be generated in ascending order.

constraints.description: Length of the Fibonacci sequence to generate

examples.explanation: Fibonacci sequence up to the 5th number
Fibonacci sequence up to the 8th number

hints.text: Review the Fibonacci sequence definition and understand how to generate the sequence.

editorial.title: Fibonacci Sequence Generation
editorial.intuition: The Fibonacci sequence can be generated using dynamic programming by maintaining a list of previously computed Fibonacci numbers.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute-force approach would involve computing each Fibonacci number recursively, which is inefficient for large values of `n`.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution would involve using dynamic programming to store previously computed Fibonacci numbers and reuse them to generate the sequence.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution would involve using a bottom-up dynamic programming approach to generate the Fibonacci sequence in O(n) time complexity.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the solution can be proven by demonstrating that the generated sequence matches the expected Fibonacci sequence.
editorial.dryRun: A dry run of the solution would involve manually computing the Fibonacci sequence for a small value of `n` to ensure correctness.
commonMistakes: Incorrectly implementing the dynamic programming approach
